passages.title: Farmers
passages.source_html:
Farmers


An iconic chain store in New Zealand reaches a milestone


A


The chain of stores known as Farmers began one hundred years ago as a mail order company. Today the Farmers chain employs 3,500 staff across 58 branches making it the most venerable institution of its type in New Zealand. 'Coming to Farmers once a week has become woven into the fabric of New Zealand culture,' says business historian lan Hunter. By 1960, one in every ten people had an account with the company. It was not only a shop but also hosted the largest annual parade in the country, the Santa Parade each Christmas time. Gary Blumenthal, whose family have been customers for three generations, understands well the role Farmers have played in New Zealand culture. 'We were on holiday in Auckland,' he explains, 'and I decided that the lookout tower on top of the Farmers’ building would be a unique place to fit the ring on my new fiancée’s finger.' The couple has been married for 50 years.


B


Farmers' longevity can be attributed to the philosophy fostered by Robert Laidlaw, the Scottish immigrant who established the business. Right from the outset, Laidlaw guaranteed customer satisfaction, and those who were not entirely happy with a purchase would receive a full refund with no questions asked. This was certainly the first time such a guarantee had been made by a New Zealand retailer and was probably one of the first worldwide. Laidlaw believed that the scheme could work if he ensured that every product sold was of the highest quality relative to its price.


C


Another important aspect of the Farmers' philosophy was to internationalize the New Zealand market. By the time the Hobson Street store opened in 1920 in order to sell directly to customers for the first time, Laidlaw had established buying offices in New York and London so as to offer his customers the latest designs. This was a bold move in those days and won Farmers a devoted following. It's also true that whoever you speak to regarding Farmers in the early years, whether managers or workers, they all emphasize how the company represented a large family. Everyone employed by Laidlaw felt they were building something that would ultimately be to the benefit of the whole country. For example, to mark the centenary celebrations, 58 Farmers' stores around the country announced the neighborhood charities they would raise funds for everything from guide dog services to volunteer fire brigades will benefit.


D


Farmers has always had a heart, according to former board chairman Rawdon Busfield. 'One day I was in the Hobson Street store and a woman came in with two small children – they didn't have a lot of money, you could tell. That week we had a special on, a big bar of chocolate for a shilling... But the boy only had a penny. I took the penny and gave him the chocolate.' However, not everything has stayed the same. Farmers was once home to genteel tea rooms, children's playgrounds, and an annual sale to celebrate the birthday of Hector the Parrot – who came to embody the store in the eyes of many shoppers and who died, aged 131, in the 1970s. You could buy houses from Farmers, and its saddle factory supplied the armed forces. In fact, although the company has grown considerably over the years, today it sells a narrower assortment of products than in the past.


E


Walk through a Farmers store today and you'll see big changes. Its most recent branch in the suburb of Albany is filled with bright lights and big brands. The branch sells cosmetics from 16 different international companies and buys from approximately 500 suppliers, of which about 30 percent are locally owned. 'Eight or ten years ago,' says current chief executive Rod McDermott, 'lots of brands wouldn't partner with us. We weren't in trouble as such, but we could have been doing much better because we were focused too much on price and not on fashion. The reorientation of the last decade has seen a great improvement.'


F


Farmers almost didn't pull through one economic crisis. In the mid 1980s, business was booming and the company had stores throughout the country. Then, with sales topping $375 million, it was taken over by Chase Corporation. Lincoln Laidlaw, the son of the company's founder, remembers the dark days following the stock market crash of 1987 and the collapse of the Chase Corporation. 'The business was being divided up,' he says, 'and I believe that the original culture of the store was dispelled and it hasn't been recovered. That's a shame, but the business is still strong.' For a few turbulent years the stores were controlled first by a consortium of Australian banks, and later a local corporation. In 2003, it went back into 'family' ownership with the purchase by the James Pascoe Group owned by an established New Zealand retail family. Brand power has made Farmers profitable again, according to McDermott. 'And now we're becoming the brand we used to be... It's like the rebirth of an icon.'

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 - 18


Look at the six paragraphs (A - F). Which paragraph contains the following information?

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 a mention of the company being sold
✅ 正确答案：F

定位信息：

company being sold


对应原文：

F段， “Then, with sales topping $375 million, it was taken over by Chase Corporation... For a few turbulent years the stores were controlled first by a consortium of Australian banks, and later a local corporation.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到公司被出售的提及。F段明确提到公司被Chase Corporation接管，后又被澳大利亚银行联盟和一家当地公司控制。这与题目要求相符，因此答案是F。
15

15 a reference to how the range of goods for sale has become more limited
✅ 正确答案：D

定位信息：

goods, more limited


对应原文：

D段，“In fact, although the company has grown considerably over the years, today it sells a narrower assortment of products than in the past.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到公司商品范围变得更有限的提及。D段提到尽管公司多年来发展壮大，但如今的商品种类比过去更少。这与题目要求相符，因此答案是D。
16

16 an example of an innovative offer made by the company's founder
✅ 正确答案：B

定位信息：

innovative offer, founder


对应原文：

B段，Right from the outset, Laidlaw guaranteed customer satisfaction, and those who were not entirely happy with a purchase would receive a full refund with no questions asked. This was certainly the first time such a guarantee had been made by a New Zealand retailer and was probably one of the first worldwide.


解题思路：

题目要求找到创始人提供创新优惠的例子。B段提到Laidlaw从一开始就保证客户满意，不满意的购买者可获得无条件退款。这在新西兰甚至全世界都是第一次。
17

17 a description of a romantic event on the shop's roof
✅ 正确答案：A

定位信息：

romantic event, shop's roof


对应原文：

A段，“We were on holiday in Auckland,' he explains, 'and I decided that the lookout tower on top of the Farmers building would be a unique place to fit the ring on my new fiancée’s finger.'”


解题思路：

题目要求找到商店屋顶上发生的浪漫事件的描述。A段描述了一对情侣在农民公司大楼的瞭望塔上订婚，这是一个浪漫的事件，因此答案是A。
18

18 a description of the latest store in the chain
✅ 正确答案：E

定位信息：

latest store, description


对应原文：

E段，“Walk through a Farmers store today and you'll see big changes. Its most recent branch in the suburb of Albany is filled with bright lights and big brands.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到连锁店最新一家店的描述。E段描述了农民公司在Albany的最新分店，这符合题目要求，因此答案是E。

Questions 19 - 22


Complete the sentences. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS in each gap.

19

Farmers was set up as a/an business before it became a chain.

正确答案： 19 mail order
Question 19

定位信息：

business, before, chain


对应原文：

A段，“The chain of stores known as Farmers began one hundred years ago as a mail order company.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到Farmers最初的商业形式。原文A段提到Farmers开始作为一家邮购公司。因此答案是mail order。

20

Robert Laidlaw set up overseas so he could sell up-to-date fashions.

正确答案： 20 buying offices
Question 20

定位信息：

Robert Laidlaw, overseas, fashions


对应原文：

C段，“Laidlaw had established buying officers in New York and London so as to offer his customers the latest designs.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到Laidlaw在海外建立了什么。C段提到Laidlaw在纽约和伦敦设立了进货中心（buying offices）。因此答案是buying offices。

21

According to employees, Farmers was like a/an .

正确答案： 21 large family##family
Question 21

定位信息：

Farmers was like a/an…


对应原文：

C段，“It's also true that whoever you speak to regarding Farmers in the early years, whether managers or workers, they all emphasize how the company represented a large family.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到员工如何看待Farmers公司。C段提到公司员工将公司视为一个大家庭，因此答案是large family。

22

Farmers held a/an once a year in honour of the company's famous pet.

正确答案： 22 sale
Question 22

定位信息：

Farmers held a/an… once a year


对应原文：

D段，“…an annual sale to celebrate the birthday of Hector the Parrot.”


解题思路：

题目要求找到Farmers每年举行的活动。D段提到Farmers每年举行一次促销活动庆祝Hector the Parrot的生日，因此答案是sale。题目中once a year对应原文中的annual

Questions 23 - 26


Choose the correct person (A - F) for each idea.


List of Ideas

A The character of the company has changed for the worse.

B The store is selling more local products than in the past.

C The company believes in offering lower prices than their competitors.

D The staff has always believed in generosity.

E The store is an integral part of the local way of life.

F The store needed to change its approach to business.


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
23

23 lan Hunter
✅ 正确答案：E

定位信息：

lan Hunter


对应原文：

A段，“Coming to Farmers once a week has become woven into the fabric of New Zealand culture,' says business historian lan Hunter.”


解题思路：

题目要求匹配lan Hunter的观点。A段提到他认为来Farmers购物已成为新西兰文化的一部分，与选项E相符。
24

24 Rawdon Busfield
✅ 正确答案：D

定位信息：

Rawdon Busfield


对应原文：

D段，“Farmers has always had a heart, according to former board chairman Rawdon Busfield. 'One day I was in the Hobson Street store and a woman came in with two small children – they didn't have a lot of money, you could tell. That week we had a special on, a big bar of chocolate for a shilling... But the boy only had a penny. I took the penny and gave him the chocolate.'”


解题思路：

题目要求匹配Rawdon Busfield的观点。D段提到Rawdon Busfield认为Farmers的员工总是心地善良，乐于助人，这与选项D相符。
25

25 Rod McDermott
✅ 正确答案：F

定位信息：

Rod McDermott


对应原文：

E段，“'Eight or ten years ago,' says current chief executive Rod McDermott, 'lots of brands wouldn't partner with us. We weren't in trouble as such, but we could have been doing much better because we were focused too much on price and not on fashion. The reorientation of the last decade has seen a great improvement.'”


解题思路：

题目要求匹配Rod McDermott的观点。E段提到Rod McDermott认为商店需要改变经营策略，选项F符合这一描述，因此答案是F。
26

26 Lincoin Laidlaw
✅ 正确答案：A

定位信息：

Lincoln Laidlaw


对应原文：

F段，“'The business was being divided up,' he says, 'and I believe that the original culture of the store was dispelled and it hasn't been recovered. That's a shame, but the business is still strong.'”


解题思路：

题目要求匹配Lincoln Laidlaw的观点。F段提到Lincoln Laidlaw认为公司的文化变差了，选项A符合这一描述，因此答案是A。
